description: This figure shows a bright-field transmission electron microscopy (TEM) micrograph of 0.02Nb medium-Mn low-density steel (nominal composition Fe-12Mn-9Al-3Cr-1.4C-0.02Nb) with a 500 nm scale bar, paired with an inset selected area electron diffraction (SAED) pattern. The main micrograph displays a submicron NbC precipitate (marked as Spot 1) dispersed in the austenite (γ) matrix. The indexed SAED pattern verifies the identity of the precipitate as NbC, and confirms a cube-on-cube orientation relationship between the austenite matrix and NbC phase: [100]γ // [100]NbC, with indexed diffraction spots including 0\(\bar{2}\)2, 002, 0\(\bar{2}\)0 for NbC, and 0\(\bar{1}\)1, 0\(\bar{1}\)\(\bar{1}\) for γ phase. This characterization directly validates the existence of NbC precipitates in the studied steel, supporting the proposed mechanism that NbC precipitation contributes to grain refinement and additional strengthening in Nb-alloyed medium-Mn low-density steels.
